title: Yang's Conjecture
context_text: Gao and Yang constructed a planar graph $G$ with $c_1(G) \geq 4$, showing that allowing only one cop to move per turn can push the cop number above 3 even for planar graphs. Yang conjectured that permitting at most two cops to move simultaneously is enough to restore the classical bound of 3 cops for all planar graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves that $c_2(G) \leq 3$ for every planar graph $G$, confirming Yang's conjecture and simultaneously strengthening the classical theorem of Aigner and Fromme that $c(G) \leq 3$ for planar graphs.
notes: The introduction text is truncated before the full conjecture statement is shown; the statement is reconstructed from the abstract and surrounding context. Reference [17] is a 2018 conference talk by Boting Yang.
paper_title: Guarding isometric subgraphs and Cops and Robber in planar graphs
paper_authors: Sebastián González Hermosillo de la Maza
